title: Doug Beason
content:
{{Top|rwp}}
{{Person
|image=[[File:Dougbeason.jpg]]
|name=Doug Beason
|birth=[[1950s|1953]]<ref name="SF Encyclopedia">{{WebCite|url=https://sf-encyclopedia.com/entry/beason_doug|text=Beason, Doug|work=The Encyclopedia of Science Fiction|archivedate=20250814085848}}</ref>
|death=
|listedpronouns=<!--Only include pronouns if they have been explicitly specified by the individual to which this article pertains.-->
|nationality=American
|occupation=*Writer<ref name="Wordfire">{{WebCite|url=https://wordfirepress.com/collections/doug-beason?srsltid=AfmBOoof68kPqn2DEFgnmj6y44F_wHo0LSMoc3cS9Gf_LdFWgk6M4P_1|text=Doug Beason|work=Wordfire Press|archivedate=20260222092555}}</ref>

'''Doug Beason''' (born [[1950s|1953]]<ref name="SF Encyclopedia" /> and credited as '''J. Douglas Beason''' when writing non-fiction),<ref name="Official Site">{{OfficialSite|url=https://https://www.dougbeason.com/|text=Official site|archivedate=20250422000003}}</ref> is an American scientist and science fiction author. In [[1995]], he wrote ''[[When the Desert Wind Turns: The Stormtrooper's Tale]]'' for the short-story collection ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]''.

Beason is a [[1977]] graduate of the [[Wikipedia:United States Air Force Academy|United States Air Force Academy]], and he later received a Ph.D. in Physics at the [[Wikipedia:University of New Mexico|University of New Mexico]].<ref name="About">{{WebCite|url=https://www.dougbeason.com/about|text=About Doug|work=Dougbeason.com|archivedate=20250422001401}}</ref> He has served as Chief Scientist for the [[Wikipedia:United States Air Force|USAF]] [[Wikipedia:United States Space Command|Space Command]] and on the [[Wikipedia:White House|White House]] science staff for [[Wikipedia:President of the United States|US Presidents]] [[Wikipedia:Bill Clinton|Bill Clinton]] and [[Wikipedia:George W. Bush|George W. Bush]].<ref name="Bent">{{WebCite|url=https://www.thebentagency.com/doug-beason|text=Doug Beason|work=The Bent Agency|archivedate=20260222092528}}</ref>

As an author, Beason has written numerous sci-fi and thriller novels, non-fiction books, articles, and short stories. He has collaborated with [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and [[Wikipedia:Ben Bova|Ben Bova]], been nominated for a [[Wikipedia:Nebula Award|Nebula Award]] for the novel ''[[Wikipedia:Assemblers of Infinity|Assemblers of Infinity]]'', and been awarded the [[Wikipedia:National Defense University (United States)|National Defense University]] President's "Strategic Vision" award. The book that garnered that award, ''Science and Technology Policy for the post-Cold War: A Case for Long-Term Research'', was used as a textbook at the [[Wikipedia:National War College|National War College]] and the [[Wikipedia:USAF Air War College|USAF Air War College]].<ref name="About" /> In [[2019]], he released his seventeenth book.<ref name="Seventeen">{{WebCite|url=https://www.castlepinesconnection.com/doug-beason-releases-17th-book/|text=Doug Beason releases 17th book|work=Castle Pines Connection|date=2019-10-01|archivedate=20210916193300}}</ref>
